data.title: Trade détente with China leads to slow rollout of Trump’s chip levies
data.description:
President Donald Trump’s administration has decided not to impose new tariffs on semiconductor imports from China until June 2027, despite accusing Beijing of violating trade rules in the global chip market. The delay follows a quiet trade ceasefire that Trump and Chinese President Xi Jinping reached in October during a meeting in South Korea, according […]
source: https://www.cryptopolitan.com/trade-detente-with-china-trumps-chip-levies/

data.title: Telegram crypto black markets now make about $2 billion a month for China-based users
data.description:
Telegram now runs the largest crypto black-market economy ever documented, with Chinese-speaking users transacting literally $2 billion every month. According to an Elliptic’s investigation released this week, simple access to Telegram, the ability to relaunch banned channels, and Chinese-language moderation have replaced the technical barriers that once defined darknet crime. Elliptic estimates that one of […]
source: https://www.cryptopolitan.com/telegram-crypto-black-markets-china-users/

data.title: Ethereum Is Emerging as a Global Public Good – and That Changes How It Should Be Valued, Says William Mougayar
data.description:
Ethereum is no longer just another blockchain competing on speed or transaction fees. Instead, it is increasingly functioning as an Internet-scale public good — a shift that demands a fundamentally different way of thinking about value.
source: https://cryptonews.com/news/ethereum-is-emerging-as-a-global-public-good-and-that-changes-how-it-should-be-valued-says-william-mougayar/

data.title: IMF says El Salvador in talks to sell state-run Chivo Bitcoin wallet
data.description:

source: https://cointelegraph.com/news/imf-el-salvador-chivo-bitcoin-wallet?utm_source=rss_feed&utm_medium=rss&utm_campaign=rss_partner_inbound

data.title: January Fed Rate Cut Odds Hit All-Time Low of 13% on Polymarket – Here Is Why Crypto Bets Are Fading
data.description:
The likelihood of a Federal Reserve interest rate cut in January has dropped to an all-time low of just 13% according to data from Polymarket. This significant decrease in expectations highlights a shift in market sentiment regarding monetary policy and interest rates. In addition, the fervor surrounding cryptocurrency betting has noticeably diminished, with investors increasingly cautious and reevaluating their positions in light of broader economic conditions and regulatory factors. This fading enthusiasm for crypto-related wagers suggests a growing uncertainty in the market, as participants grapple with the implications of potential interest rate decisions and their impact on digital asset performance.
source: https://blocknews.com/january-fed-rate-cut-odds-hit-all-time-low-of-13-on-polymarket-here-is-why-crypto-bets-are-fading/

data.title: Russia to recognize crypto as ‘currency asset’ and open market for all investors
data.description:
Russian authorities intend to treat cryptocurrencies and stablecoins as “currency assets,” according to the Bank of Russia’s new plan for complete regulation of the market. For the first time, the monetary authority is now proposing to significantly expand investor access to decentralized digital money, although not without fine print. Russian regulators open door for cryptocurrency […]
source: https://www.cryptopolitan.com/russia-to-recognize-crypto-as-currency-asset/

data.title: China’s rising exports to Germany widen deficit to record levels
data.description:
Germany’s trade deficit with China is heading toward a record in 2025 as demand for German products keeps shrinking. Analysts tracking trade flows say the gap is widening fast and shows no sign of easing soon. If the trend holds, it could fuel protectionist pressure inside Europe’s largest economy at a time when political patience […]
source: https://www.cryptopolitan.com/chinas-rising-exports-germany-widen-deficit/

data.title: Bitcoin May Stay Outside Central Bank Vaults, Billionaire Says
data.description:
According to recent interviews, billionaire investor Ray Dalio has sharpened his caution about Bitcoin’s fit for official reserves while still recognizing its scarce nature. Related Reading: Before You Sell Bitcoin For Gold, Hear This Warning He said that Bitcoin carries money-like qualities because of its limited supply, but he drew a firm line over who should hold it on a balance sheet. Dalio said public transaction records and the risk of outside interference make it hard for reserve managers to treat Bitcoin the same way they treat gold. Dalio Flags Traceability Concerns Dalio warned that the open ledger that underpins Bitcoin creates vulnerabilities for large custodians
source: https://www.newsbtc.com/news/bitcoin/bitcoin-may-stay-outside-central-bank-vaults-billionaire-says/

data.title: EU Backs Digital Euro Holding Caps to Protect Banking Stability
data.description:
The Council of the European Union has formally backed the European Central Bank’s proposal to impose caps on individual holdings of a future digital euro, marking a key step forward in shaping the legal framework for the central bank digital currency.
source: https://www.ethnews.com/eu-backs-digital-euro-holding-caps-to-protect-banking-stability/
